questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: Consider the following statements regarding India's recent bilateral trade engagements: 1. India has recently concluded a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) with the United Kingdom. 2. The formal negotiations for the India-UK Free Trade Agreement were concluded after three years of formal talks. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. India and the United Kingdom (UK) have announced the conclusion of a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) following three years of formal negotiations.
questions.takeaway: The India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) was concluded after three years of formal negotiations.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: India and the United Kingdom (UK) announced the conclusion of a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) after three years of formal talks.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: Recently, India announced the conclusion of a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) following three years of formal negotiations. This agreement was concluded with which of the following economies?
questions.explanation: India and the United Kingdom (UK) have announced the conclusion of a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) after three years of formal talks. The distractors represent other major economies with which India has either negotiated or is currently negotiating trade agreements.
questions.takeaway: India's active engagement in bilateral trade agreements recently culminated in the conclusion of an FTA with the United Kingdom after a three-year negotiation period.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: India and the United Kingdom (UK) announced the conclusion of a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) after three years of formal talks.
